column3: A document that records information about one or more entities. Can contain free text, incorporate images, and be composed of ordered parts. Parts are themselves simple_document instances, allowing figures, sections, and composite reports to be modelled within the same structure. Document type distinguishes structural role (e.g. figure, working note, report section); domain type distinguishes subject matter (e.g. x-radiography, provenance).
Document Note: Simple Document Model Overview

column1: Document Note: Simple Document Model Overview
column3: This model defines a reusable document entity that can represent anything from a single figure caption to a multi-section technical report. A document can incorporate images directly and can be composed of ordered part-documents. Creation provenance is captured via the document_creation model and subsequent updates via the document_update model. The model is intentionally generic; source-specific typing and content are applied through the domain type and document type controlled lists.
The entity or entities this document is about. Can be a heritage object, sample, examination event, or any other CRM entity. One document may document multiple entities.
The creation event for this document.

An update event recording a change to the textual content of this document. Each update captures the editor responsible, the scale of their involvement, and the timespan of their activity. The current text is held via P190 on this document; the full history of updates is recoverable via the sequence of associated E13 update events.
An image directly incorporated into this document. For figure-type documents this is the primary content. For other document types images may be incorporated alongside text.

An ordered part of this document. Parts are themselves simple_document instances. A figure part incorporates an image and carries a caption as its document text. Parts may have their own creation provenance and type.

An optional link to another version of this document. Related versions are expected to share the same document title but carry different version numbers and unique system identifiers. Uses P130_shows_features_of as a pragmatic approximation for version succession; a more precise versioning property may be adopted in future. Version links are undirected in the sense that both older and newer versions can be linked from the same document.
E41: Unique System Label or ID

column1: E41: Unique System Label or ID
column3: Required within some documentation or database systems, such as ResearchSpace required label.
The display label or title of the document.
The textual content of the document. For figure parts this carries the caption. May be absent for purely structural container documents.
The structural role of this document. Selected from a controlled list: working_note, report_section, figure, composite_report, catalogue_entry.

The subject domain of this document. Selected from a controlled list: e.g. x-radiography, infrared_reflectography, entry_text, provenance, condition, treatment, support, paint_layers, surface_coatings, bibliography, organic_analysis, cross_section.

A dimension node representing the ordinal position of a specific part within its parent document. The numeric value is not stored directly on this node; a separate literal structure would be required to record it.

A conceptual object acting as a positional node linking this document to one of its parts. Carries the ordinal position of the part within this specific parent document via an associated E54 dimension. Each parent-part relationship has its own E28 instance, allowing the same part to appear in multiple parent documents at different positions.

An optional dimension node recording the version number of this document. Intended for documents where changes are significant enough to warrant explicit versioning rather than simple updates. The version number can be updated via the document_update model using P43_has_dimension as the target property. When a new version is recorded, a link to the previous version should be made via P130_shows_features_of.
